directions.name: Restore the accepted-correct surface and try a light 4-column serpentine CTA swizzle on the hot-band grid
directions.hypothesis: Warp-local fragment-residency experiments have now failed correctness twice, so the next safe branch should move away from register choreography and toward cache locality. The user's L2 idea is the cleanest option: restore the accepted-correct implementation surface first, then apply a very light logical CTA swizzle that only reorders hot-band block_col mapping inside small groups. That keeps the inner microkernel untouched and tests whether the scheduler can get a little more B-tile locality from a serpentine launch order.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Inter-CTA L2 locality across neighboring hot-band B tiles rather than warp-local tensor scheduling.
directions.risk: Moderate. The restore is low-risk, and the swizzle is intentionally lightweight, but the earlier heavier grouped traversal was negative.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if the swizzle leaves L2 throughput flat or regresses runtime on the restored surface.

directions.name: Restore the accepted-correct surface and try warp-specialized Pg2s staging without changing the tile shape
directions.hypothesis: If cache-locality remains flat, the next remaining human-idea family is Pg2s orchestration. Even on the accepted-correct surface, all eight warps still share staging duties in the hot-band loop. A minimal producer/consumer split that leaves the tile shape and export path alone would test whether some tensor issue is still being diluted by CTA-wide staging work.
directions.expected_bottleneck: CTA-level staging orchestration and barrier dilution inside the hot-band loop.
directions.risk: High. Producer/consumer split can easily hurt tensor issue or blow up synchronization if the ownership boundary is wrong.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if barrier falls but tensor activity and main-kernel time do not improve.

directions.name: Restore-only fallback to the accepted best surface
directions.hypothesis: If the swizzle or producer/staging paths do not validate quickly, the safest move is to stay on the fastest known correct implementation instead of keeping the recent incorrect experiments live.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Not a bottleneck attack; this is the fallback reset path.
directions.stop_condition: Use this if the higher-ranked directions fail validation or correctness.

notes: Round 10/50 pivots away from warp-local fragment-residency experiments because they have been too fragile on correctness. The recommended next move is therefore dir_01: restore the accepted-correct surface and test a light L2-friendly CTA swizzle, which is the safest remaining human-idea branch with a plausible upside.
